title: lesquerella arctica subsp. purshii
intro: Lesquerella arctica subsp. purshii, also known as Pursh's bladderpod, is a plant species in the mustard family (Brassicaceae) that is native to western North America. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The health benefits of Lesquerella arctica subsp. purshii include reducing inflammation and improving wound healing, as well as potentially lowering cholesterol levels and reducing the risk of cardiovascular disease. The plant can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and capsules, and has also been used in topical applications such as creams and salves to take advantage of its medicinal properties.
